_directive_meta.note: Phase 4 sub-group definitions. Three sub-groups cover all 22 terms. Verse-level routing (Phase 6/7) assigns individual P-status verses to the sub-group matching their inner-being content. Most terms span multiple sub-groups at verse level — term primary assignment reflects the dominant inner-being function of the term.

subgroups.full_description: The distortion of abundance — Material wealth, fatness, and abundance produce systematic inner-being distortions when received without wisdom and divine ground: the heart grows dull and unresponsive to God (sha.men verb), complacency breeds contempt and moral blindness (sha.a.nan), the rich self-assess falsely (plousios), riches choke the word and become an object of misplaced trust (ploutos, o.sher), wealth declares itself innocent while concealing wickedness (a.shar, a.shir), and the inner person becomes constitutionally unable to say enough (hon). This is not a condemnation of wealth but a mapping of its systematic inner-being risks.

primary_terms.note: VCGs 4697-001 and 4697-002: making fat of heart as judgment; spiritual fatness of prosperity turning heart from God
VCG 413-001: complacent ease producing contempt and moral blindness
VCG 1142-001: magnitude of iniquity and spiritual failure as the weight of distortion
Primary NT term for the distorted rich (Luk 6:24; Luk 18:23; Rev 3:17; Jam 5:1; Jam 2:6)
Self-directed becoming rich (Luk 12:21 negative; Rev 3:17; 1Cor 4:8; 1Tim 6:9)
Riches as choking force and object of misplaced trust (Mat 13:22; Mar 4:19; Jam 5:2)
The rich as morally distorted — wise in own eyes, full of violence (Pro 28:11; Mic 6:12)
Riches as object of misplaced trust (Pro 11:28); riches kept to owner's hurt
Drive to become rich as snare (1Tim 6:9); self-declaration of wealth as false moral vindication (Hos 12:8)
Insatiability face: never says enough (Pro 30:15); false rich/false poor (Pro 13:7)

subgroups.full_description: Wealth as moral category and wisdom-domain — The biblical wisdom tradition treats wealth as a moral category whose quality is determined by the inner character of the person who holds it. Wealth from righteousness is treasure; wealth from wickedness brings trouble (cho.sen). Wisdom carries riches and righteousness together (o.sher, hon). The soul of the diligent is richly supplied (da.shen). Character — diligence, trust, righteousness — determines whether wealth is blessing or snare. The domain is not morally neutral — it reveals and tests the inner person.

primary_terms.note: VCG 681-001: wealth as moral category — character determines whether wealth becomes blessing or snare. Primary M46-B term.
VCG 7010-001: wealth in relation to inner-being orientation and moral condition (Proverbs wisdom register)
Wisdom-moral uses: Pro 8:18 (Wisdom carries riches and righteousness); Pro 22:4 (fruit of humility and fear); Ecc 9:11 (unpredictability)
Wisdom observations: Pro 22:2 (rich and poor before God); Ruth 3:10
Wisdom commands: Pro 23:4 (do not toil to be rich); Pro 10:4 (diligent hand)
VCG 111-001: soul of diligent richly supplied; generosity produces enrichment; trust produces enrichment
Fatness as marker of accumulated human power under divine judgment
Rich/fat as descriptor with moral weight (Hab 1:16 conquest's rich food; Isa 30:23 eschatological plenty)
Richly — ordered abundance from God (1Tim 6:17)
Ordering faces: Jam 2:5 poor rich in faith; 1Tim 6:17 instruction to rich; 2Cor 8:9 Christ rich
Ordered uses: 2Cor 8:2 wealth of generosity; Heb 11:26 Moses counting reproach greater wealth

subgroups.full_description: The ordered inner response — The inner-being resolution of the wealth domain lies beyond wealth itself: contentment as a learned disposition independent of material circumstance (autarkēs), the secure quietness of divinely-grounded ease (sha.a.nan 413-002), consecration over self-accumulation (cha.san), joy in God's provision (a.dan), poverty of spirit as the inner posture of dependence before God (ptōchos), and the Christological paradigm of voluntary impoverishment that enriches others (ptōcheuō, ploutizō). The ordered person is neither destroyed by poverty nor corrupted by abundance.

primary_terms.note: VCG 4898-001: contentment as learned inner disposition. Primary M46-C term. Phil 4:11.
VCG 413-002: divinely intended secure quietness — the ease of God's people in shalom
VCG 7109-001: negated — consecration over self-accumulation; not-hoarding as ordered disposition (Isa 23:18)
VCG 3836-001: delighting in God's goodness — inner joy in divine provision (Neh 9:25)
VCG 111-002: anointing as divine blessing (routed to M39-A — BOUNDARY designation in M46-C)
Christ becoming poor as voluntary relinquishment for others' enrichment (2Cor 8:9)
Poverty of spirit (Mat 5:3); poor as heirs of the kingdom (Jam 2:5; Luk 6:20); Zacchaeus restitution (Luk 19:8)
Ordered uses: rich toward God (Luk 12:21 positive); rich in good works (1Tim 6:18); 2Cor 8:9
Enriching others (2Cor 6:10 — as poor yet making many rich; 2Cor 9:11 — enriched to be generous)
Ordered faces: Rev 2:9 Smyrna poor but rich; 2Cor 8:9 Christ who was rich

boundary_notes.note: The anointing group (111-002, Psa 23:5) is routed to M39-A via cluster_subgroup_id. In M46, da.shen's anointing face is designated BOUNDARY under M46-C. The prosperity face (111-001) is M46-B primary.

sha.a.nan spans M46-A (413-001 complacent ease) and M46-C (413-002 divine secure quietness). Term primary home is M46-A; 413-002 verses route to M46-C. The R117 peace findings remain as cross-cluster enrichment, not contamination.

rov is a BOUNDARY term in M46 — it names the magnitude of inner-moral realities (iniquity, steadfast love) rather than a wealth characteristic per se. Assigned M46-A as the sub-group where magnitude of distortion is most operative. DIM-152-SD001 remains open for Session D.

plousios, ploutos, plouteō, ptōchos span all three sub-groups at verse level. The primary assignment reflects the dominant inner-being function of the term's verse pool. Individual verses route to the sub-group matching their specific inner-being content.

nine_unresolved_UT_verses.note: 9 borderline UT verses remain unresolved from Phase 2. These are not assigned to sub-groups in this directive. They require researcher resolution before Phase 6 routing can include them.
nine_unresolved_UT_verses.status: Pending researcher decision — flagged in Phase 3 session notes
